evals.prompt:
Here are all the components we've developed through the Dunford positioning process. Please build me the completed positioning canvas.

Product: Janna Systems CRM
Market category: CRM for investment banks
Competitive alternatives: (1) spreadsheets + email for tracking relationships (primary), (2) Siebel/generic enterprise CRM (secondary)
Unique attributes: multi-hop relationship modeling, relationship strength scoring, Bloomberg Terminal integration
Value themes: (1) relationship capital visibility — know your firm's entire network at a glance, (2) warm path discovery — find the fastest intro route to any target executive, (3) compliance-grade audit trail for relationship-based communications
Who cares a lot: investment banks (deal sourcing depends on relationship access), firms managing large deal pipelines, senior bankers who work primarily on relationship-driven revenue
Relevant trend: none identified
evals.expected_output: The skill should produce a clean, complete positioning canvas in structured table format with all five components populated. Should run quality checks and surface any gaps. Should note the 'no trend' decision as intentional. Should include next steps: sales story, messaging document, roadmap review. Should not require the user to do additional work — the canvas should be immediately shareable.

assertions.name: produces-complete-canvas
assertions.description: Canvas includes all five components: market category, competitive alternatives, unique attributes, value, who cares a lot

assertions.name: uses-table-format
assertions.description: Canvas is structured as a table showing the relationship between alternatives → attributes → value → who cares

assertions.name: includes-quality-checks
assertions.description: Runs at least the consistency check, proof check, and sales readiness check

assertions.name: includes-next-steps
assertions.description: Provides a clear list of what to build next from the canvas

evals.prompt: We want to document our positioning but we haven't done the full Dunford process. Here's what we know: we sell a direct-to-consumer product sampling platform. Our competitors are traditional in-store sampling programs. Our unique feature is digital targeting — we can deliver samples to specific consumers based on demographics and interests. Can you build us a positioning canvas from this?
evals.expected_output: The skill should proceed but flag multiple gaps: competitive alternatives are incomplete (only primary listed, no 'do nothing' or digital alternatives), value has not been translated from the attribute (digital targeting → ?), 'who cares a lot' is not specified, market category is not stated. Should build a partial canvas with available information and clearly mark the gaps as [INCOMPLETE] or [VALIDATE], then ask for the missing pieces or recommend completing the earlier steps.

assertions.name: flags-incomplete-components
assertions.description: Explicitly identifies which components are missing or underdeveloped rather than inventing content to fill gaps

assertions.name: builds-partial-canvas
assertions.description: Produces a canvas with what is available rather than refusing entirely

assertions.name: recommends-specific-gaps-to-fill
assertions.description: Tells the user specifically what is missing and what earlier step would address it

evals.prompt: Build me a positioning canvas for our product. Here are the details: we make project management software.
evals.expected_output: The skill should refuse to build a canvas from this description alone. 'We make project management software' provides no competitive alternatives, no unique attributes, no value, no target customer characteristics, and no market category distinction. The skill should explain that a positioning canvas captures deliberate choices, not category membership, and ask the user to either provide the components or run through the positioning workshop first.

assertions.name: refuses-to-fabricate
assertions.description: Does not invent competitive alternatives, attributes, or value claims when the user provides insufficient input

assertions.name: explains-what-is-needed
assertions.description: Clearly explains what information is required to build a meaningful canvas

assertions.name: offers-path-forward
assertions.description: Offers to run the full positioning workshop or guides the user to provide the missing components
